name: The Credit Repair Attorneys
description_short: Attorney-led credit repair company offering dispute letters, creditor validation, and debt settlement negotiation to help clients remove negative items from credit reports.
description_long:
The Credit Repair Attorneys was founded in 2015 by Matthew Adams, Esq., who drew inspiration from his own experience struggling with student loan and credit card debt during law school. Adams worked as a front-line negotiator at a debt negotiation law firm, where he gained insider knowledge of debt collection agency tactics and strategies. He founded the company after observing that most credit repair firms operated without legal oversight or adequate client information protection, prompting him to build a practice centered on attorney-led service delivery and fiduciary responsibility.

The company offers credit restoration services including dispute letters sent to credit bureaus, validation letters to creditors, monthly progress reports, and debt settlement negotiation with creditors. Monthly plans start at $99.99 with customized options available. The service targets individuals dealing with collection accounts, inaccurate credit reporting, and harassing collection calls. Their stated mission is to help clients resolve inaccurate bureau reporting and eliminate collection harassment while they focus on family and career obligations.

The company differentiates itself by emphasizing attorney-led operations and fiduciary protection of client information. Their website explicitly positions the attorney requirement as a legal obligation to maintain client data securely, contrasting this with non-attorney credit repair firms. They highlight diverse experience serving varied clientele, knowledge of current evolving credit laws, and a strong base of repeat clients. The founder's personal debt experience and years working inside collection agencies inform their approach.

While the company presents legitimate credentials and services, important caveats exist. The website explicitly disclaims that using the site does not form an attorney-client relationship, and all pricing plans are described as invitations to offer requiring representative contact for final terms. No specific attorney qualifications, licensing details, client reviews, or performance metrics are provided on the website. The company's liability limitations and informational-only positioning suggest consumers should verify credentials and obtain detailed written agreements before committing.
pros: Founded and operated by a licensed attorney (Matthew Adams, Esq.) with prior experience negotiating against debt collection agencies
Explicitly positions fiduciary protection of client information as a legal requirement, differentiating from non-attorney credit repair companies
Offers comprehensive services including dispute letters, validation letters, settlement negotiation, and monthly progress reports
Founder has direct insider experience working as a debt negotiator, providing knowledge of collection agency tactics and industry practices
Customized pricing plans starting at $99.99 monthly with ability to tailor service to individual needs
Long operational history since 2015 with reported base of repeat clients
Free consultation available to prospective clients
cons: Website explicitly disclaims formation of attorney-client relationship through site usage, limiting legal protection claims
No specific attorney licensing numbers, credentials, or verification details provided on website
All pricing plans described as 'invitations to offer' requiring representative contact for binding terms—actual costs and commitments unclear
No client testimonials, case results, or performance metrics published on website to verify effectiveness
Broad language about 'many different types of credit issues' without detailing specific capabilities or limitations
best_for: Consumers with collection accounts seeking attorney-backed dispute and validation processes
Individuals wanting to negotiate settlements with creditors while maintaining professional representation
People concerned about information security and preferring attorney-managed versus non-attorney credit repair services
Those experiencing collection harassment who want legal intervention alongside credit restoration
diagnosis: The Credit Repair Attorneys is best for consumers with collection accounts or inaccurate negative items on their credit reports who specifically value attorney-led representation and want fiduciary protection of their personal information. The primary caveat is that the website lacks verifiable credentials, client proof of success, and binding service details—prospective clients must contact the company directly to confirm attorney licensing, understand actual costs, and review written service agreements before engagement.
services: Monthly progress reports on credit file changes
Ongoing client representation and dedicated team support
